method: 设计内容感知的压缩策略，根据图像内容特征动态调整编码与解码过程中的参数和处理方式。
result: 实验表明该方法在主观视觉质量上与最先进的端到端压缩方法及经典方法具有竞争力。
conclusion: 考虑图像内容差异的压缩方法能更有效平衡压缩率与画质，为后续研究提供新方向。
search_text: Content-oriented learned image compression 面向内容的学习图像压缩 In recent years, with the development of deep neural networks, end-to-end optimized image compression has made significant progress and exceeded the classic methods in terms of rate-distortion performance. However, most learning-based image compression methods are unlabeled and do not consider image semantics or content when optimizing the model. In fact, human eyes have different sensitivities to different content, so the image content also needs to be considered. In this paper, we propose a content-oriented image compression method, which handles different kinds of image contents with different strategies. Extensive experiments show that the proposed method achieves competitive subjective results compared with state-of-the-art end-to-end learned image compression methods or classic methods. 近年来，随着深度神经网络的发展，端到端优化图像压缩取得了重大进展，在率失真性能方面超越了经典方法。然而，大多数基于学习的图像压缩方法都是无标签的，并且在优化模型时不考虑图像语义或内容。事实上，人眼对不同的内容有不同的敏感度，因此图像内容也需要考虑。在本文中，我们提出了一种面向内容的图像压缩方法，该方法用不同的策略处理不同类型的图像内容。大量的实验表明，与最先进的端到端学习图像压缩方法或经典方法相比，所提出的方法取得了有竞争力的主观结果。 本文提出了一种面向内容的图像压缩方法，通过针对不同图像内容采用不同策略优化压缩效果。 Meng Li,Shangyin Gao,Yihui Feng,Yibo Shi,Jing Wang cs.CV,cs.MM,eess.IV

authors_text: Guijie Zhu,Zhun Fan,Jiacheng Liu,Duan Yuan,Peili Ma,Meihua Wang,Weihua Sheng,Kelvin C. P. Wang
title: RHA-Net: An Encoder-Decoder Network with Residual Blocks and Hybrid Attention Mechanisms for Pavement Crack Segmentation
title_zh: RHA-Net：具有残差块和混合注意力机制的编码器-解码器网络，用于路面裂缝分割
abstract: The acquisition and evaluation of pavement surface data play an essential role in pavement condition evaluation. In this paper, an efficient and effective end-to-end network for automatic pavement crack segmentation, called RHA-Net, is proposed to improve the pavement crack segmentation accuracy. The RHA-Net is built by integrating residual blocks (ResBlocks) and hybrid attention blocks into the encoder-decoder architecture. The ResBlocks are used to improve the ability of RHA-Net to extract high-level abstract features. The hybrid attention blocks are designed to fuse both low-level features and high-level features to help the model focus on correct channels and areas of cracks, thereby improving the feature presentation ability of RHA-Net. An image data set containing 789 pavement crack images collected by a self-designed mobile robot is constructed and used for training and evaluating the proposed model. Compared with other state-of-the-art networks, the proposed model achieves better performance and the functionalities of adding residual blocks and hybrid attention mechanisms are validated in a comprehensive ablation study. Additionally, a light-weighted version of the model generated by introducing depthwise separable convolution achieves better a performance and a much faster processing speed with 1/30 of the number of U-Net parameters. The developed system can segment pavement crack in real-time on an embedded device Jetson TX2 (25 FPS). The video taken in real-time experiments is released at https://youtu.be/3XIogk0fiG4.

abstract_zh: 路面数据的采集和评价在路面状况评价中起着至关重要的作用。本文提出了一种高效且有效的自动路面裂缝分割端到端网络，称为RHA-Net，以提高路面裂缝分割精度。 RHA-Net 是通过将残差块（ResBlocks）和混合注意力块集成到编码器-解码器架构中而构建的。 ResBlocks 用于提高 RHA-Net 提取高级抽象特征的能力。混合注意力块旨在融合低级特征和高级特征，帮助模型聚焦于正确的通道和裂缝区域，从而提高RHA-Net的特征表示能力。构建了包含由自行设计的移动机器人收集的789张路面裂缝图像的图像数据集，并用于训练和评估所提出的模型。与其他最先进的网络相比，所提出的模型实现了更好的性能，并且添加残差块和混合注意机制的功能在全面的消融研究中得到了验证。此外，通过引入深度可分离卷积生成的模型的轻量级版本以 U-Net 参数数量的 1/30 实现了更好的性能和更快的处理速度。所开发的系统可以在嵌入式设备 Jetson TX2 (25 FPS) 上实时分割路面裂缝。实时实验中拍摄的视频发布于 https://youtu.be/3XIogk0fiG4。

AI.tldr: 提出RHA-Net网络用于路面裂缝分割，通过残差块和混合注意力机制提升分割精度。
AI.motivation: 路面裂缝检测需高精度分割方法，现有模型存在特征提取不足和注意力机制单一的问题。
AI.method: 基于编码器-解码器架构，集成残差块增强特征提取能力，并设计混合注意力模块融合多级特征，采用深度可分离卷积实现轻量化。
AI.result: 在789张路面图像数据集上优于现有模型，轻量化版本参数量为U-Net的1/30，Jetson TX2设备上实现25 FPS实时分割。
AI.conclusion: RHA-Net通过残差结构和混合注意力机制有效提升裂缝分割性能，轻量化设计验证了模型的实用价值。

authors_text: Mohamed Karim Belaid,Eyke Hüllermeier,Maximilian Rabus,Ralf Krestel
title: Do We Need Another Explainable AI Method? Toward Unifying Post-hoc XAI Evaluation Methods into an Interactive and Multi-dimensional Benchmark
title_zh: 我们需要另一种可解释的人工智能方法吗？将事后 XAI 评估方法统一为交互式多维基准
abstract: In recent years, Explainable AI (xAI) attracted a lot of attention as various countries turned explanations into a legal right. xAI allows for improving models beyond the accuracy metric by, e.g., debugging the learned pattern and demystifying the AI's behavior. The widespread use of xAI brought new challenges. On the one hand, the number of published xAI algorithms underwent a boom, and it became difficult for practitioners to select the right tool. On the other hand, some experiments did highlight how easy data scientists could misuse xAI algorithms and misinterpret their results. To tackle the issue of comparing and correctly using feature importance xAI algorithms, we propose Compare-xAI, a benchmark that unifies all exclusive functional testing methods applied to xAI algorithms. We propose a selection protocol to shortlist non-redundant functional tests from the literature, i.e., each targeting a specific end-user requirement in explaining a model. The benchmark encapsulates the complexity of evaluating xAI methods into a hierarchical scoring of three levels, namely, targeting three end-user groups: researchers, practitioners, and laymen in xAI. The most detailed level provides one score per test. The second level regroups tests into five categories (fidelity, fragility, stability, simplicity, and stress tests). The last level is the aggregated comprehensibility score, which encapsulates the ease of correctly interpreting the algorithm's output in one easy to compare value. Compare-xAI's interactive user interface helps mitigate errors in interpreting xAI results by quickly listing the recommended xAI solutions for each ML task and their current limitations. The benchmark is made available at https://karim-53.github.io/cxai/
summary: In recent years, Explainable AI (xAI) attracted a lot of attention as various countries turned explanations into a legal right. xAI allows for improving models beyond the accuracy metric by, e.g., debugging the learned pattern and demystifying the AI's behavior. The widespread use of xAI brought new challenges. On the one hand, the number of published xAI algorithms underwent a boom, and it became difficult for practitioners to select the right tool. On the other hand, some experiments did highlight how easy data scientists could misuse xAI algorithms and misinterpret their results. To tackle the issue of comparing and correctly using feature importance xAI algorithms, we propose Compare-xAI, a benchmark that unifies all exclusive functional testing methods applied to xAI algorithms. We propose a selection protocol to shortlist non-redundant functional tests from the literature, i.e., each targeting a specific end-user requirement in explaining a model. The benchmark encapsulates the complexity of evaluating xAI methods into a hierarchical scoring of three levels, namely, targeting three end-user groups: researchers, practitioners, and laymen in xAI. The most detailed level provides one score per test. The second level regroups tests into five categories (fidelity, fragility, stability, simplicity, and stress tests). The last level is the aggregated comprehensibility score, which encapsulates the ease of correctly interpreting the algorithm's output in one easy to compare value. Compare-xAI's interactive user interface helps mitigate errors in interpreting xAI results by quickly listing the recommended xAI solutions for each ML task and their current limitations. The benchmark is made available at https://karim-53.github.io/cxai/
abstract_zh: 近年来，随着各国将解释权转变为合法权利，可解释人工智能（xAI）引起了广泛关注。 xAI 可以通过调试学习的模式并揭开人工智能行为的神秘面纱等方式来改进模型，使其超出准确性指标。 xAI的广泛应用带来了新的挑战。一方面，已发布的 xAI 算法数量激增，从业者很难选择合适的工具。另一方面，一些实验确实凸显了数据科学家如何容易滥用 xAI 算法并误解其结果。为了解决比较和正确使用特征重要性 xAI 算法的问题，我们提出了 Compare-xAI，这是一个统一了应用于 xAI 算法的所有专有功能测试方法的基准。我们提出了一种选择协议，用于从文献中列出非冗余功能测试，即每个测试都针对解释模型时的特定最终用户需求。该基准将评估 xAI 方法的复杂性封装为三个级别的分层评分，即针对三个最终用户群体：xAI 的研究人员、从业者和外行人员。最详细的级别为每个测试提供一个分数。第二级将测试重新分为五类（保真度、脆弱性、稳定性、简单性和压力测试）。最后一级是综合可理解性分数，它将正确解释算法输出的难易程度封装在一个易于比较的值中。 Compare-xAI 的交互式用户界面通过快速列出针对每个 ML 任务推荐的 xAI 解决方案及其当前限制，有助于减少解释 xAI 结果时的错误。该基准可在 https://karim-53.github.io/cxai/ 上获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出Compare-xAI基准框架，旨在统一后验可解释AI（XAI）评估方法，通过多维评分和交互式界面解决XAI工具选择困难及误用问题。
AI.motivation: 随着XAI算法激增，实践者难以选择合适工具，且存在算法误用风险，亟需标准化评估体系以指导正确使用。
AI.method: 构建Compare-xAI基准，通过筛选非冗余功能测试，按用户群体（研究者/实践者/非专业用户）设计三级评分体系，并分类为五个维度（保真度、脆弱性等），提供交互式推荐界面。
AI.result: 开发了可交互的评估平台，用户可通过任务类型快速获取推荐XAI方案及其局限性，基准框架已开源。
AI.conclusion: Compare-xAI通过结构化评估体系有效降低XAI误用风险，为不同用户群体提供定制化算法选择依据，推动XAI方法的规范化应用。

The last level is the aggregated comprehensibility score, which encapsulates the ease of correctly interpreting the algorithm's output in one easy to compare value. Compare-xAI's interactive user interface helps mitigate errors in interpreting xAI results by quickly listing the recommended xAI solutions for each ML task and their current limitations. The benchmark is made available at https://karim-53.github.io/cxai/ 近年来，随着各国将解释权转变为合法权利，可解释人工智能（xAI）引起了广泛关注。 xAI 可以通过调试学习的模式并揭开人工智能行为的神秘面纱等方式来改进模型，使其超出准确性指标。 xAI的广泛应用带来了新的挑战。一方面，已发布的 xAI 算法数量激增，从业者很难选择合适的工具。另一方面，一些实验确实凸显了数据科学家如何容易滥用 xAI 算法并误解其结果。为了解决比较和正确使用特征重要性 xAI 算法的问题，我们提出了 Compare-xAI，这是一个统一了应用于 xAI 算法的所有专有功能测试方法的基准。我们提出了一种选择协议，用于从文献中列出非冗余功能测试，即每个测试都针对解释模型时的特定最终用户需求。该基准将评估 xAI 方法的复杂性封装为三个级别的分层评分，即针对三个最终用户群体：xAI 的研究人员、从业者和外行人员。最详细的级别为每个测试提供一个分数。第二级将测试重新分为五类（保真度、脆弱性、稳定性、简单性和压力测试）。最后一级是综合可理解性分数，它将正确解释算法输出的难易程度封装在一个易于比较的值中。 Compare-xAI 的交互式用户界面通过快速列出针对每个 ML 任务推荐的 xAI 解决方案及其当前限制，有助于减少解释 xAI 结果时的错误。该基准可在 https://karim-53.github.io/cxai/ 上获取 本文提出Compare-xAI基准框架，旨在统一后验可解释AI（XAI）评估方法，通过多维评分和交互式界面解决XAI工具选择困难及误用问题。 Mohamed Karim Belaid,Eyke Hüllermeier,Maximilian Rabus,Ralf Krestel cs.SE,cs.AI

authors_text: Heidy Khlaaf,Pamela Mishkin,Joshua Achiam,Gretchen Krueger,Miles Brundage
title: A Hazard Analysis Framework for Code Synthesis Large Language Models
title_zh: 代码合成大型语言模型的危害分析框架
abstract: Codex, a large language model (LLM) trained on a variety of codebases, exceeds the previous state of the art in its capacity to synthesize and generate code. Although Codex provides a plethora of benefits, models that may generate code on such scale have significant limitations, alignment problems, the potential to be misused, and the possibility to increase the rate of progress in technical fields that may themselves have destabilizing impacts or have misuse potential. Yet such safety impacts are not yet known or remain to be explored. In this paper, we outline a hazard analysis framework constructed at OpenAI to uncover hazards or safety risks that the deployment of models like Codex may impose technically, socially, politically, and economically. The analysis is informed by a novel evaluation framework that determines the capacity of advanced code generation techniques against the complexity and expressivity of specification prompts, and their capability to understand and execute them relative to human ability.
summary: Codex, a large language model (LLM) trained on a variety of codebases, exceeds the previous state of the art in its capacity to synthesize and generate code. Although Codex provides a plethora of benefits, models that may generate code on such scale have significant limitations, alignment problems, the potential to be misused, and the possibility to increase the rate of progress in technical fields that may themselves have destabilizing impacts or have misuse potential. Yet such safety impacts are not yet known or remain to be explored. In this paper, we outline a hazard analysis framework constructed at OpenAI to uncover hazards or safety risks that the deployment of models like Codex may impose technically, socially, politically, and economically. The analysis is informed by a novel evaluation framework that determines the capacity of advanced code generation techniques against the complexity and expressivity of specification prompts, and their capability to understand and execute them relative to human ability.
abstract_zh: Codex 是一种在各种代码库上进行训练的大型语言模型 (LLM)，其合成和生成代码的能力超越了以前的最先进技术。尽管 Codex 提供了很多好处，但可能生成如此规模的代码的模型具有显着的局限性、对齐问题、被滥用的可能性，以及提高技术领域进展速度的可能性，而这些技术领域本身可能会产生不稳定的影响或具有滥用的可能性。然而，此类安全影响尚不清楚或有待探索。在本文中，我们概述了 OpenAI 构建的危害分析框架，以揭示 Codex 等模型的部署可能在技术、社会、政治和经济方面带来的危害或安全风险。该分析以新颖的评估框架为基础，该框架根据规范提示的复杂性和表现力确定高级代码生成技术的能力，以及相对于人类能力理解和执行这些技术的能力。

AI.tldr: 构建用于评估代码合成大语言模型潜在风险的危害分析框架
AI.motivation: 现有代码生成模型如Codex虽具强大能力，但存在安全隐患、对齐问题及被滥用风险，需系统分析其技术、社会、政治和经济层面的潜在危害
AI.method: 设计评估框架测试模型处理复杂性/表达性提示的能力，并对比人类执行能力，结合多维度危害分析方法
AI.result: 识别出代码生成模型在技术可靠性、社会影响、政治安全及经济冲击等维度的潜在风险，量化模型能力与人类水平的差距
AI.conclusion: 危害分析框架可有效识别模型风险，未来需加强安全对齐机制并建立动态风险评估体系

authors_text: Tingying He,Petra Isenberg,Raimund Dachselt,Tobias Isenberg
title: BeauVis: A Validated Scale for Measuring the Aesthetic Pleasure of Visual Representations
title_zh: BeauVis：用于测量视觉表现美感的经过验证的量表
comment: ef:IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics 29(1), 2023
comments: ef:IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics 29(1), 2023
abstract: We developed and validated a rating scale to assess the aesthetic pleasure (or beauty) of a visual data representation: the BeauVis scale. With our work we offer researchers and practitioners a simple instrument to compare the visual appearance of different visualizations, unrelated to data or context of use. Our rating scale can, for example, be used to accompany results from controlled experiments or be used as informative data points during in-depth qualitative studies. Given the lack of an aesthetic pleasure scale dedicated to visualizations, researchers have mostly chosen their own terms to study or compare the aesthetic pleasure of visualizations. Yet, many terms are possible and currently no clear guidance on their effectiveness regarding the judgment of aesthetic pleasure exists. To solve this problem, we engaged in a multi-step research process to develop the first validated rating scale specifically for judging the aesthetic pleasure of a visualization (osf.io/fxs76). Our final BeauVis scale consists of five items, "enjoyable," "likable," "pleasing," "nice," and "appealing." Beyond this scale itself, we contribute (a) a systematic review of the terms used in past research to capture aesthetics, (b) an investigation with visualization experts who suggested terms to use for judging the aesthetic pleasure of a visualization, and (c) a confirmatory survey in which we used our terms to study the aesthetic pleasure of a set of 3 visualizations.
summary: We developed and validated a rating scale to assess the aesthetic pleasure (or beauty) of a visual data representation: the BeauVis scale. With our work we offer researchers and practitioners a simple instrument to compare the visual appearance of different visualizations, unrelated to data or context of use. Our rating scale can, for example, be used to accompany results from controlled experiments or be used as informative data points during in-depth qualitative studies. Given the lack of an aesthetic pleasure scale dedicated to visualizations, researchers have mostly chosen their own terms to study or compare the aesthetic pleasure of visualizations. Yet, many terms are possible and currently no clear guidance on their effectiveness regarding the judgment of aesthetic pleasure exists. To solve this problem, we engaged in a multi-step research process to develop the first validated rating scale specifically for judging the aesthetic pleasure of a visualization (osf.io/fxs76). Our final BeauVis scale consists of five items, "enjoyable," "likable," "pleasing," "nice," and "appealing." Beyond this scale itself, we contribute (a) a systematic review of the terms used in past research to capture aesthetics, (b) an investigation with visualization experts who suggested terms to use for judging the aesthetic pleasure of a visualization, and (c) a confirmatory survey in which we used our terms to study the aesthetic pleasure of a set of 3 visualizations.
abstract_zh: 我们开发并验证了一个评估视觉数据表示的审美愉悦（或美感）的评级量表：BeauVis 量表。通过我们的工作，我们为研究人员和从业者提供了一个简单的工具来比较不同可视化的视觉外观，与数据或使用上下文无关。例如，我们的评级量表可用于伴随对照实验的结果，或在深入的定性研究中用作信息数据点。由于缺乏专门针对可视化的审美愉悦量表，研究人员大多选择自己的术语来研究或比较可视化的审美愉悦。然而，许多术语都是可能的，并且目前对于它们在审美愉悦判断方面的有效性还没有明确的指导。为了解决这个问题，我们进行了多步骤的研究过程，开发了第一个经过验证的评级量表，专门用于判断可视化的审美乐趣（osf.io/fxs76）。我们最终的 BeauVis 量表由五个项目组成：“令人愉快的”、“讨人喜欢的”、“令人愉悦的”、“漂亮的”和“有吸引力的”。除了这个量表本身之外，我们还贡献了（a）对过去研究中用于捕捉美学的术语的系统回顾，（b）与可视化专家的调查，他们提出了用于判断可视化的审美愉悦的术语，以及（c）一项验证性调查，其中我们使用我们的术语来研究一组 3 个可视化的审美愉悦。

AI.tldr: 开发并验证BeauVis量表以评估可视化表示的审美愉悦
AI.motivation: 缺乏专门针对可视化的审美愉悦量表，研究者多自行选择术语，缺乏统一指导
AI.method: 多步骤研究：系统综述、专家术语建议及确认性调查验证量表
AI.result: 提出包含五个项目的BeauVis量表，并验证其有效性，同时贡献了术语综述和专家调查结果
AI.conclusion: 开发出首个验证的可视化审美愉悦量表，为研究者提供统一评估工具，并系统整理相关术语和专家建议

Given the lack of an aesthetic pleasure scale dedicated to visualizations, researchers have mostly chosen their own terms to study or compare the aesthetic pleasure of visualizations. To solve this problem, we engaged in a multi-step research process to develop the first validated rating scale specifically for judging the aesthetic pleasure of a visualization (osf.io/fxs76). Beyond this scale itself, we contribute (a) a systematic review of the terms used in past research to capture aesthetics, (b) an investigation with visualization experts who suggested terms to use for judging the aesthetic pleasure of a visualization, and (c) a confirmatory survey in which we used our terms to study the aesthetic pleasure of a set of 3 visualizations. 我们开发并验证了一个评估视觉数据表示的审美愉悦（或美感）的评级量表：BeauVis 量表。通过我们的工作，我们为研究人员和从业者提供了一个简单的工具来比较不同可视化的视觉外观，与数据或使用上下文无关。例如，我们的评级量表可用于伴随对照实验的结果，或在深入的定性研究中用作信息数据点。由于缺乏专门针对可视化的审美愉悦量表，研究人员大多选择自己的术语来研究或比较可视化的审美愉悦。然而，许多术语都是可能的，并且目前对于它们在审美愉悦判断方面的有效性还没有明确的指导。为了解决这个问题，我们进行了多步骤的研究过程，开发了第一个经过验证的评级量表，专门用于判断可视化的审美乐趣（osf.io/fxs76）。我们最终的 BeauVis 量表由五个项目组成：“令人愉快的”、“讨人喜欢的”、“令人愉悦的”、“漂亮的”和“有吸引力的”。除了这个量表本身之外，我们还贡献了（a）对过去研究中用于捕捉美学的术语的系统回顾，（b）与可视化专家的调查，他们提出了用于判断可视化的审美愉悦的术语，以及（c）一项验证性调查，其中我们使用我们的术语来研究一组 3 个可视化的审美愉悦。 开发并验证BeauVis量表以评估可视化表示的审美愉悦 Tingying He,Petra Isenberg,Raimund Dachselt,Tobias Isenberg cs.HC,cs.GR

authors_text: Jerin Paul Selvan,Pravin S. Game
title: Playing a 2D Game Indefinitely using NEAT and Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 使用 NEAT 和强化学习无限玩 2D 游戏
abstract: For over a decade now, robotics and the use of artificial agents have become a common thing.Testing the performance of new path finding or search space optimization algorithms has also become a challenge as they require simulation or an environment to test them.The creation of artificial environments with artificial agents is one of the methods employed to test such algorithms.Games have also become an environment to test them.The performance of the algorithms can be compared by using artificial agents that will behave according to the algorithm in the environment they are put in.The performance parameters can be, how quickly the agent is able to differentiate between rewarding actions and hostile actions.This can be tested by placing the agent in an environment with different types of hurdles and the goal of the agent is to reach the farthest by taking decisions on actions that will lead to avoiding all the obstacles.The environment chosen is a game called "Flappy Bird".The goal of the game is to make the bird fly through a set of pipes of random heights.The bird must go in between these pipes and must not hit the top, the bottom, or the pipes themselves.The actions that the bird can take are either to flap its wings or drop down with gravity.The algorithms that are enforced on the artificial agents are NeuroEvolution of Augmenting Topologies (NEAT) and Reinforcement Learning.The NEAT algorithm takes an "N" initial population of artificial agents.They follow genetic algorithms by considering an objective function, crossover, mutation, and augmenting topologies.Reinforcement learning, on the other hand, remembers the state, the action taken at that state, and the reward received for the action taken using a single agent and a Deep Q-learning Network.The performance of the NEAT algorithm improves as the initial population of the artificial agents is increased.
summary: For over a decade now, robotics and the use of artificial agents have become a common thing.Testing the performance of new path finding or search space optimization algorithms has also become a challenge as they require simulation or an environment to test them.The creation of artificial environments with artificial agents is one of the methods employed to test such algorithms.Games have also become an environment to test them.The performance of the algorithms can be compared by using artificial agents that will behave according to the algorithm in the environment they are put in.The performance parameters can be, how quickly the agent is able to differentiate between rewarding actions and hostile actions.This can be tested by placing the agent in an environment with different types of hurdles and the goal of the agent is to reach the farthest by taking decisions on actions that will lead to avoiding all the obstacles.The environment chosen is a game called "Flappy Bird".The goal of the game is to make the bird fly through a set of pipes of random heights.The bird must go in between these pipes and must not hit the top, the bottom, or the pipes themselves.The actions that the bird can take are either to flap its wings or drop down with gravity.The algorithms that are enforced on the artificial agents are NeuroEvolution of Augmenting Topologies (NEAT) and Reinforcement Learning.The NEAT algorithm takes an "N" initial population of artificial agents.They follow genetic algorithms by considering an objective function, crossover, mutation, and augmenting topologies.Reinforcement learning, on the other hand, remembers the state, the action taken at that state, and the reward received for the action taken using a single agent and a Deep Q-learning Network.The performance of the NEAT algorithm improves as the initial population of the artificial agents is increased.
abstract_zh: 十多年来，机器人技术和人工代理的使用已经成为一件常见的事情。测试新的寻路或搜索空间优化算法的性能也成为一个挑战，因为它们需要模拟或环境来测试它们。用人工代理创建人工环境是测试此类算法的方法之一。游戏也成为测试它们的环境。可以通过使用人工代理来比较算法的性能，这些人工代理将根据所处环境中的算法进行行为。性能参数可以是代理运行的速度。能够区分奖励行为和敌意行为。这可以通过将代理放置在具有不同类型障碍的环境中进行测试，代理的目标是通过做出避免所有障碍的行动决策来到达最远的地方。选择的环境是一个名为“Flappy Bird”的游戏。游戏的目标是让小鸟飞过一组随机高度的管道。小鸟必须进入这些管道之间，并且不能撞到顶部、底部或管道本身。动作鸟可以采取的方法是拍打翅膀或通过重力落下。对人工智能体强制执行的算法是增强拓扑的神经进化（NEAT）和强化学习。NEAT 算法采用“N”个初始人工智能体群体。它们通过考虑目标函数、交叉、突变和增强拓扑来遵循遗传算法。另一方面，强化学习会记住状态、在该状态下采取的行动以及使用单代理和深度 Q 学习网络。NEAT 算法的性能随着人工代理初始群体的增加而提高。

AI.tldr: 本文通过NEAT算法和强化学习训练人工代理在《Flappy Bird》游戏中无限游玩，以测试路径寻找和优化算法的性能。
AI.motivation: 随着机器人和人工智能代理的发展，需要通过模拟环境（如游戏）验证新算法的性能，尤其是其在复杂障碍中的决策能力。
AI.method: 采用NEAT算法（基于遗传算法的拓扑结构进化）和深度Q学习网络（DQN）的强化学习方法，通过增加初始代理种群规模优化NEAT性能。
AI.result: 实验表明，随着NEAT初始种群规模的增加，代理在游戏中的障碍规避能力和生存距离显著提升。
AI.conclusion: NEAT与强化学习结合可有效训练代理完成复杂游戏任务，且NEAT性能受初始种群规模影响显著。

authors_text: Arrasy Rahman,Elliot Fosong,Ignacio Carlucho,Stefano V. Albrecht
title: Generating Teammates for Training Robust Ad Hoc Teamwork Agents via Best-Response Diversity
title_zh: 通过最佳响应多样性生成队友来训练强大的临时团队合作代理
comment: Accepted in Transactions of Machine Learning Research
comments: Accepted in Transactions of Machine Learning Research
abstract: Ad hoc teamwork (AHT) is the challenge of designing a robust learner agent that effectively collaborates with unknown teammates without prior coordination mechanisms. Early approaches address the AHT challenge by training the learner with a diverse set of handcrafted teammate policies, usually designed based on an expert's domain knowledge about the policies the learner may encounter. However, implementing teammate policies for training based on domain knowledge is not always feasible. In such cases, recent approaches attempted to improve the robustness of the learner by training it with teammate policies generated by optimising information-theoretic diversity metrics. The problem with optimising existing information-theoretic diversity metrics for teammate policy generation is the emergence of superficially different teammates. When used for AHT training, superficially different teammate behaviours may not improve a learner's robustness during collaboration with unknown teammates. In this paper, we present an automated teammate policy generation method optimising the Best-Response Diversity (BRDiv) metric, which measures diversity based on the compatibility of teammate policies in terms of returns. We evaluate our approach in environments with multiple valid coordination strategies, comparing against methods optimising information-theoretic diversity metrics and an ablation not optimising any diversity metric. Our experiments indicate that optimising BRDiv yields a diverse set of training teammate policies that improve the learner's performance relative to previous teammate generation approaches when collaborating with near-optimal previously unseen teammate policies.

Early approaches address the AHT challenge by training the learner with a diverse set of handcrafted teammate policies, usually designed based on an expert's domain knowledge about the policies the learner may encounter. However, implementing teammate policies for training based on domain knowledge is not always feasible. In such cases, recent approaches attempted to improve the robustness of the learner by training it with teammate policies generated by optimising information-theoretic diversity metrics. The problem with optimising existing information-theoretic diversity metrics for teammate policy generation is the emergence of superficially different teammates. When used for AHT training, superficially different teammate behaviours may not improve a learner's robustness during collaboration with unknown teammates. In this paper, we present an automated teammate policy generation method optimising the Best-Response Diversity (BRDiv) metric, which measures diversity based on the compatibility of teammate policies in terms of returns. We evaluate our approach in environments with multiple valid coordination strategies, comparing against methods optimising information-theoretic diversity metrics and an ablation not optimising any diversity metric. Our experiments indicate that optimising BRDiv yields a diverse set of training teammate policies that improve the learner's performance relative to previous teammate generation approaches when collaborating with near-optimal previously unseen teammate policies.
abstract_zh: 临时团队合作（AHT）是设计一个强大的学习代理的挑战，该代理可以在没有事先协调机制的情况下与未知的队友有效地协作。早期的方法通过使用一组不同的手工制定的队友策略来培训学习者来解决 AHT 挑战，这些策略通常是根据专家关于学习者可能遇到的策略的领域知识而设计的。然而，实施基于领域知识的队友培训政策并不总是可行的。在这种情况下，最近的方法试图通过使用优化信息论多样性指标生成的队友策略来训练学习器，从而提高学习器的鲁棒性。优化队友策略生成的现有信息论多样性指标的问题是表面上不同的队友的出现。当用于 AHT 训练时，表面上不同的队友行为可能不会提高学习者在与未知队友协作期间的鲁棒性。在本文中，我们提出了一种优化最佳响应多样性（BRDiv）指标的自动化队友策略生成方法，该方法根据队友策略在回报方面的兼容性来衡量多样性。我们在具有多种有效协调策略的环境中评估我们的方法，与优化信息论多样性度量的方法和不优化任何多样性度量的消融方法进行比较。我们的实验表明，优化 BRDiv 会产生一组多样化的训练队友策略，当与之前未见过的近乎最优的队友策略协作时，相对于之前的队友生成方法，这些策略可以提高学习者的表现。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于最佳响应多样性的队友策略生成方法，以提升对抗性团队协作中学习者的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖领域知识设计队友策略或优化信息论多样性指标，但生成的表面多样性队友可能无法有效提升学习者与未知队友协作的鲁棒性。
AI.method: 通过优化最佳响应多样性（BRDiv）指标生成队友策略，该指标基于队友策略在收益上的兼容性衡量多样性。
AI.result: 实验表明，优化BRDiv生成的队友策略在面对近似最优未知队友时，显著优于信息论多样性指标方法及无多样性优化的基线。
AI.conclusion: BRDiv指标能生成更具行为差异性的训练队友，有效提升学习者在对抗性团队协作场景中的鲁棒性表现。

authors_text: Shaolun Ruan,Yong Wang,Weiwen Jiang,Ying Mao,Qiang Guan
title: VACSEN: A Visualization Approach for Noise Awareness in Quantum Computing
title_zh: VACSEN：量子计算中噪声感知的可视化方法
abstract: Quantum computing has attracted considerable public attention due to its exponential speedup over classical computing. Despite its advantages, today's quantum computers intrinsically suffer from noise and are error-prone. To guarantee the high fidelity of the execution result of a quantum algorithm, it is crucial to inform users of the noises of the used quantum computer and the compiled physical circuits. However, an intuitive and systematic way to make users aware of the quantum computing noise is still missing. In this paper, we fill the gap by proposing a novel visualization approach to achieve noise-aware quantum computing. It provides a holistic picture of the noise of quantum computing through multiple interactively coordinated views: a Computer Evolution View with a circuit-like design overviews the temporal evolution of the noises of different quantum computers, a Circuit Filtering View facilitates quick filtering of multiple compiled physical circuits for the same quantum algorithm, and a Circuit Comparison View with a coupled bar chart enables detailed comparison of the filtered compiled circuits. We extensively evaluate the performance of VACSEN through two case studies on quantum algorithms of different scales and an in-depth interviews with 12 quantum computing users. The results demonstrate the effectiveness and usability of VACSEN in achieving noise-aware quantum computing.
summary: Quantum computing has attracted considerable public attention due to its exponential speedup over classical computing. Despite its advantages, today's quantum computers intrinsically suffer from noise and are error-prone. To guarantee the high fidelity of the execution result of a quantum algorithm, it is crucial to inform users of the noises of the used quantum computer and the compiled physical circuits. However, an intuitive and systematic way to make users aware of the quantum computing noise is still missing. In this paper, we fill the gap by proposing a novel visualization approach to achieve noise-aware quantum computing. It provides a holistic picture of the noise of quantum computing through multiple interactively coordinated views: a Computer Evolution View with a circuit-like design overviews the temporal evolution of the noises of different quantum computers, a Circuit Filtering View facilitates quick filtering of multiple compiled physical circuits for the same quantum algorithm, and a Circuit Comparison View with a coupled bar chart enables detailed comparison of the filtered compiled circuits. We extensively evaluate the performance of VACSEN through two case studies on quantum algorithms of different scales and an in-depth interviews with 12 quantum computing users. The results demonstrate the effectiveness and usability of VACSEN in achieving noise-aware quantum computing.
abstract_zh: 量子计算由于其相对于经典计算的指数级加速而引起了公众的广泛关注。尽管具有诸多优点，但当今的量子计算机本质上会受到噪声的影响并且容易出错。为了保证量子算法执行结果的高保真度，告知用户所使用的量子计算机和编译的物理电路的噪声至关重要。然而，仍然缺乏一种直观、系统的方法来让用户意识到量子计算噪声。在本文中，我们通过提出一种新颖的可视化方法来实现噪声感知量子计算来填补这一空白。它通过多个交互式协调视图提供量子计算噪声的整体视图：具有类似电路设计的计算机演化视图概述了不同量子计算机噪声的时间演化，电路过滤视图有助于快速过滤同一量子算法的多个编译物理电路，带有耦合条形图的电路比较视图可以对过滤后的编译电路进行详细比较。我们通过两个不同规模量子算法的案例研究以及对 12 位量子计算用户的深入访谈，对 VACSEN 的性能进行了广泛的评估。结果证明了 VACSEN 在实现噪声感知量子计算方面的有效性和可用性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为VACSEN的可视化方法，用于提高量子计算中的噪声意识。
AI.motivation: 量子计算虽然具有显著优势，但当前的量子计算机存在噪声和误差问题。为了确保量子算法执行结果的高保真度，需要向用户明确展示量子计算机和编译后的物理电路的噪声情况。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种新的可视化方法，通过多个交互式协调视图实现噪声感知量子计算。包括：计算机演化视图、电路过滤视图和电路比较视图。
AI.result: 通过两个不同规模的量子算法案例研究和与12位量子计算用户的深入访谈，验证了VACSEN在实现噪声感知量子计算方面的有效性和可用性。
AI.conclusion: VACSEN为用户提供了一种直观和系统的方式来了解量子计算的噪声问题，有助于提高量子计算的可靠性和用户满意度。

authors_text: Liqun Huang,Long Chen,Baihai Zhang,Senchun Chai
title: A Transformer-based Generative Adversarial Network for Brain Tumor Segmentation
title_zh: 基于 Transformer 的脑肿瘤分割生成对抗网络
abstract: Brain tumor segmentation remains a challenge in medical image segmentation tasks. With the application of transformer in various computer vision tasks, transformer blocks show the capability of learning long-distance dependency in global space, which is complementary with CNNs. In this paper, we proposed a novel transformer-based generative adversarial network to automatically segment brain tumors with multi-modalities MRI. Our architecture consists of a generator and a discriminator, which are trained in min-max game progress. The generator is based on a typical "U-shaped" encoder-decoder architecture, whose bottom layer is composed of transformer blocks with resnet. Besides, the generator is trained with deep supervision technology. The discriminator we designed is a CNN-based network with multi-scale $L_{1}$ loss, which is proved to be effective for medical semantic image segmentation. To validate the effectiveness of our method, we conducted experiments on BRATS2015 dataset, achieving comparable or better performance than previous state-of-the-art methods.
summary: Brain tumor segmentation remains a challenge in medical image segmentation tasks. With the application of transformer in various computer vision tasks, transformer blocks show the capability of learning long-distance dependency in global space, which is complementary with CNNs. In this paper, we proposed a novel transformer-based generative adversarial network to automatically segment brain tumors with multi-modalities MRI. Our architecture consists of a generator and a discriminator, which are trained in min-max game progress. The generator is based on a typical "U-shaped" encoder-decoder architecture, whose bottom layer is composed of transformer blocks with resnet. Besides, the generator is trained with deep supervision technology. The discriminator we designed is a CNN-based network with multi-scale $L_{1}$ loss, which is proved to be effective for medical semantic image segmentation. To validate the effectiveness of our method, we conducted experiments on BRATS2015 dataset, achieving comparable or better performance than previous state-of-the-art methods.
abstract_zh: 脑肿瘤分割仍然是医学图像分割任务中的一个挑战。随着 Transformer 在各种计算机视觉任务中的应用，Transformer 块表现出了学习全局空间中的长距离依赖的能力，这与 CNN 是互补的。在本文中，我们提出了一种新型的基于变压器的生成对抗网络，用于通过多模态 MRI 自动分割脑肿瘤。我们的架构由生成器和鉴别器组成，它们在最小-最大游戏进度中进行训练。该生成器基于典型的“U 形”编码器-解码器架构，其底层由带有 resnet 的 Transformer 块组成。此外，生成器经过深度监控技术的训练。我们设计的判别器是一个基于 CNN 的网络，具有多尺度 $L_{1}$ 损失，被证明对于医学语义图像分割是有效的。为了验证我们方法的有效性，我们在 BRATS2015 数据集上进行了实验，取得了与之前最先进方法相当或更好的性能。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于Transformer的生成对抗网络（GAN）用于多模态MRI的脑肿瘤分割
AI.motivation: 传统CNN在捕捉长距离依赖上存在局限，而Transformer能有效学习全局信息，结合GAN提升分割精度
AI.method: 生成器采用U型结构，底部集成Transformer与ResNet模块，并应用深度监督；判别器为CNN结构，结合多尺度L1损失
AI.result: 在BRATS2015数据集上实验，性能达到或超过现有最先进方法
AI.conclusion: 证明Transformer与GAN结合在医学图像分割中的有效性，为脑肿瘤分割提供新思路

authors_text: Huy Xuan Pham,Andriy Sarabakha,Mykola Odnoshyvkin,Erdal Kayacan
title: PencilNet: Zero-Shot Sim-to-Real Transfer Learning for Robust Gate Perception in Autonomous Drone Racing
title_zh: PencilNet：零样本模拟到真实迁移学习，实现自主无人机竞赛中稳健的门感知
comment: accepted for publication by IEEE RA-L/IROS 2022
comments: accepted for publication by IEEE RA-L/IROS 2022
abstract: In autonomous and mobile robotics, one of the main challenges is the robust on-the-fly perception of the environment, which is often unknown and dynamic, like in autonomous drone racing. In this work, we propose a novel deep neural network-based perception method for racing gate detection -- PencilNet -- which relies on a lightweight neural network backbone on top of a pencil filter. This approach unifies predictions of the gates' 2D position, distance, and orientation in a single pose tuple. We show that our method is effective for zero-shot sim-to-real transfer learning that does not need any real-world training samples. Moreover, our framework is highly robust to illumination changes commonly seen under rapid flight compared to state-of-art methods. A thorough set of experiments demonstrates the effectiveness of this approach in multiple challenging scenarios, where the drone completes various tracks under different lighting conditions.
summary: In autonomous and mobile robotics, one of the main challenges is the robust on-the-fly perception of the environment, which is often unknown and dynamic, like in autonomous drone racing. In this work, we propose a novel deep neural network-based perception method for racing gate detection -- PencilNet -- which relies on a lightweight neural network backbone on top of a pencil filter. This approach unifies predictions of the gates' 2D position, distance, and orientation in a single pose tuple. We show that our method is effective for zero-shot sim-to-real transfer learning that does not need any real-world training samples. Moreover, our framework is highly robust to illumination changes commonly seen under rapid flight compared to state-of-art methods. A thorough set of experiments demonstrates the effectiveness of this approach in multiple challenging scenarios, where the drone completes various tracks under different lighting conditions.
abstract_zh: 在自主和移动机器人技术中，主要挑战之一是对环境的强大的动态感知，这通常是未知的和动态的，就像在自主无人机竞赛中一样。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新颖的基于深度神经网络的感知方法，用于赛车门检测——PencilNet——它依赖于铅笔过滤器之上的轻量级神经网络主干。这种方法将门的 2D 位置、距离和方向的预测统一在单个姿势元组中。我们证明，我们的方法对于零样本模拟到真实的迁移学习是有效的，不需要任何现实世界的训练样本。此外，与最先进的方法相比，我们的框架对于快速飞行下常见的光照变化具有高度鲁棒性。一系列彻底的实验证明了这种方法在多种具有挑战性的场景中的有效性，其中无人机在不同的照明条件下完成各种轨迹。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度神经网络的门检测方法PencilNet，用于自主无人机竞速中的鲁棒门感知，实现零样本模拟到现实迁移学习。
AI.motivation: 自主机器人面临动态未知环境下的实时感知挑战，现有方法需依赖真实训练样本或对光照变化敏感，难以适应无人机高速飞行的复杂场景。
AI.method: 设计轻量级神经网络结合铅笔滤波器，统一输出门的2D位置、距离和方向，通过零样本迁移学习无需真实世界训练数据，提升光照变化下的鲁棒性。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在多种光照条件和复杂赛道中均能有效完成门检测，优于现有技术，验证了其在自主无人机竞速中的实用性。
AI.conclusion: PencilNet通过创新网络结构和零样本迁移策略，解决了无人机竞速中动态环境感知难题，为模拟到现实的迁移学习提供了高效鲁棒的解决方案。

ai_content_json.method: 设计轻量级神经网络结合铅笔滤波器，统一输出门的2D位置、距离和方向，通过零样本迁移学习无需真实世界训练数据，提升光照变化下的鲁棒性。
ai_content_json.result: 实验表明该方法在多种光照条件和复杂赛道中均能有效完成门检测，优于现有技术，验证了其在自主无人机竞速中的实用性。
ai_content_json.conclusion: PencilNet通过创新网络结构和零样本迁移策略，解决了无人机竞速中动态环境感知难题，为模拟到现实的迁移学习提供了高效鲁棒的解决方案。

search_text: PencilNet: Zero-Shot Sim-to-Real Transfer Learning for Robust Gate Perception in Autonomous Drone Racing PencilNet：零样本模拟到真实迁移学习，实现自主无人机竞赛中稳健的门感知 In autonomous and mobile robotics, one of the main challenges is the robust on-the-fly perception of the environment, which is often unknown and dynamic, like in autonomous drone racing. In this work, we propose a novel deep neural network-based perception method for racing gate detection -- PencilNet -- which relies on a lightweight neural network backbone on top of a pencil filter. This approach unifies predictions of the gates' 2D position, distance, and orientation in a single pose tuple. We show that our method is effective for zero-shot sim-to-real transfer learning that does not need any real-world training samples. Moreover, our framework is highly robust to illumination changes commonly seen under rapid flight compared to state-of-art methods. A thorough set of experiments demonstrates the effectiveness of this approach in multiple challenging scenarios, where the drone completes various tracks under different lighting conditions. 在自主和移动机器人技术中，主要挑战之一是对环境的强大的动态感知，这通常是未知的和动态的，就像在自主无人机竞赛中一样。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新颖的基于深度神经网络的感知方法，用于赛车门检测——PencilNet——它依赖于铅笔过滤器之上的轻量级神经网络主干。这种方法将门的 2D 位置、距离和方向的预测统一在单个姿势元组中。我们证明，我们的方法对于零样本模拟到真实的迁移学习是有效的，不需要任何现实世界的训练样本。此外，与最先进的方法相比，我们的框架对于快速飞行下常见的光照变化具有高度鲁棒性。一系列彻底的实验证明了这种方法在多种具有挑战性的场景中的有效性，其中无人机在不同的照明条件下完成各种轨迹。 本文提出一种基于深度神经网络的门检测方法PencilNet，用于自主无人机竞速中的鲁棒门感知，实现零样本模拟到现实迁移学习。 Huy Xuan Pham,Andriy Sarabakha,Mykola Odnoshyvkin,Erdal Kayacan cs.RO

authors_text: Christian Kindermann,Martin G. Skjæveland
title: A Survey of Syntactic Modelling Structures in Biomedical Ontologies
title_zh: 生物医学本体中句法建模结构的调查
comment: Accepted at The 21st International Semantic Web Conference
comments: Accepted at The 21st International Semantic Web Conference
abstract: Despite the large-scale uptake of semantic technologies in the biomedical domain, little is known about common modelling practices in published ontologies. OWL ontologies are often published only in the crude form of sets of axioms leaving the underlying design opaque. However, a principled and systematic ontology development life cycle is likely to be reflected in regularities of the ontology's emergent syntactic structure. To develop an understanding of this emergent structure, we propose to reverse-engineer ontologies taking a syntax-directed approach for identifying and analysing regularities for axioms and sets of axioms. We survey BioPortal in terms of syntactic modelling trends and common practices for OWL axioms and class frames. Our findings suggest that biomedical ontologies only share simple syntactic structures in which OWL constructors are not deeply nested or combined in a complex manner. While such simple structures often account for large proportions of axioms in a given ontology, many ontologies also contain non-trivial amounts of more complex syntactic structures that are not common across ontologies.
summary: Despite the large-scale uptake of semantic technologies in the biomedical domain, little is known about common modelling practices in published ontologies. OWL ontologies are often published only in the crude form of sets of axioms leaving the underlying design opaque. However, a principled and systematic ontology development life cycle is likely to be reflected in regularities of the ontology's emergent syntactic structure. To develop an understanding of this emergent structure, we propose to reverse-engineer ontologies taking a syntax-directed approach for identifying and analysing regularities for axioms and sets of axioms. We survey BioPortal in terms of syntactic modelling trends and common practices for OWL axioms and class frames. Our findings suggest that biomedical ontologies only share simple syntactic structures in which OWL constructors are not deeply nested or combined in a complex manner. While such simple structures often account for large proportions of axioms in a given ontology, many ontologies also contain non-trivial amounts of more complex syntactic structures that are not common across ontologies.
abstract_zh: 尽管语义技术在生物医学领域被大规模采用，但人们对已发布本体中的常见建模实践知之甚少。 OWL 本体通常仅以公理集的粗略形式发布，从而使底层设计变得不透明。然而，有原则的、系统的本体发展生命周期很可能反映在本体的涌现句法结构的规律性中。为了加深对这种新兴结构的理解，我们建议采用语法导向的方法对本体进行逆向工程，以识别和分析公理和公理集的规律性。我们根据 OWL 公理和类框架的句法建模趋势和常见实践对 BioPortal 进行了调查。我们的研究结果表明，生物医学本体仅共享简单的句法结构，其中 OWL 构造函数没有以复杂的方式深度嵌套或组合。虽然这种简单的结构通常占给定本体中公理的很大比例，但许多本体还包含大量更复杂的句法结构，这些结构在本体中并不常见。

AI.tldr: 通过反向工程技术分析生物医学本体中的句法建模结构，揭示其常见模式与复杂性。
AI.motivation: 尽管语义技术在生物医学领域广泛应用，但现有本体的建模实践缺乏系统性研究，其句法结构复杂性与规律性尚不明确。
AI.method: 采用语法导向的反向工程方法，对BioPortal中的OWL公理及类框架进行系统分析，识别其句法结构规律。
AI.result: 发现生物医学本体普遍采用简单句法结构（如非嵌套的OWL构造器），但部分本体包含少量复杂结构，且这些复杂结构缺乏跨本体一致性。
AI.conclusion: 生物医学本体在句法结构上呈现简单性主导，复杂结构存在但缺乏跨本体的共性，表明建模实践尚未形成统一规范。

OWL ontologies are often published only in the crude form of sets of axioms leaving the underlying design opaque. However, a principled and systematic ontology development life cycle is likely to be reflected in regularities of the ontology's emergent syntactic structure. To develop an understanding of this emergent structure, we propose to reverse-engineer ontologies taking a syntax-directed approach for identifying and analysing regularities for axioms and sets of axioms. We survey BioPortal in terms of syntactic modelling trends and common practices for OWL axioms and class frames. While such simple structures often account for large proportions of axioms in a given ontology, many ontologies also contain non-trivial amounts of more complex syntactic structures that are not common across ontologies. 尽管语义技术在生物医学领域被大规模采用，但人们对已发布本体中的常见建模实践知之甚少。 OWL 本体通常仅以公理集的粗略形式发布，从而使底层设计变得不透明。然而，有原则的、系统的本体发展生命周期很可能反映在本体的涌现句法结构的规律性中。为了加深对这种新兴结构的理解，我们建议采用语法导向的方法对本体进行逆向工程，以识别和分析公理和公理集的规律性。我们根据 OWL 公理和类框架的句法建模趋势和常见实践对 BioPortal 进行了调查。我们的研究结果表明，生物医学本体仅共享简单的句法结构，其中 OWL 构造函数没有以复杂的方式深度嵌套或组合。虽然这种简单的结构通常占给定本体中公理的很大比例，但许多本体还包含大量更复杂的句法结构，这些结构在本体中并不常见。 通过反向工程技术分析生物医学本体中的句法建模结构，揭示其常见模式与复杂性。 Christian Kindermann,Martin G. authors_text: Martin Fajcik,Petr Motlicek,Pavel Smrz
title: Claim-Dissector: An Interpretable Fact-Checking System with Joint Re-ranking and Veracity Prediction
title_zh: 声明解析器：具有联合重排序和准确性预测功能的可解释事实检查系统
abstract: We present Claim-Dissector: a novel latent variable model for fact-checking and analysis, which given a claim and a set of retrieved evidences jointly learns to identify: (i) the relevant evidences to the given claim, (ii) the veracity of the claim. We propose to disentangle the per-evidence relevance probability and its contribution to the final veracity probability in an interpretable way -- the final veracity probability is proportional to a linear ensemble of per-evidence relevance probabilities. In this way, the individual contributions of evidences towards the final predicted probability can be identified. In per-evidence relevance probability, our model can further distinguish whether each relevant evidence is supporting (S) or refuting (R) the claim. This allows to quantify how much the S/R probability contributes to the final verdict or to detect disagreeing evidence. Despite its interpretable nature, our system achieves results competitive with state-of-the-art on the FEVER dataset, as compared to typical two-stage system pipelines, while using significantly fewer parameters. It also sets new state-of-the-art on FAVIQ and RealFC datasets. Furthermore, our analysis shows that our model can learn fine-grained relevance cues while using coarse-grained supervision, and we demonstrate it in 2 ways. (i) We show that our model can achieve competitive sentence recall while using only paragraph-level relevance supervision. (ii) Traversing towards the finest granularity of relevance, we show that our model is capable of identifying relevance at the token level. To do this, we present a new benchmark TLR-FEVER focusing on token-level interpretability -- humans annotate tokens in relevant evidences they considered essential when making their judgment. Then we measure how similar are these annotations to the tokens our model is focusing on.
summary: We present Claim-Dissector: a novel latent variable model for fact-checking and analysis, which given a claim and a set of retrieved evidences jointly learns to identify: (i) the relevant evidences to the given claim, (ii) the veracity of the claim. We propose to disentangle the per-evidence relevance probability and its contribution to the final veracity probability in an interpretable way -- the final veracity probability is proportional to a linear ensemble of per-evidence relevance probabilities. In this way, the individual contributions of evidences towards the final predicted probability can be identified. In per-evidence relevance probability, our model can further distinguish whether each relevant evidence is supporting (S) or refuting (R) the claim. This allows to quantify how much the S/R probability contributes to the final verdict or to detect disagreeing evidence. Despite its interpretable nature, our system achieves results competitive with state-of-the-art on the FEVER dataset, as compared to typical two-stage system pipelines, while using significantly fewer parameters. It also sets new state-of-the-art on FAVIQ and RealFC datasets. Furthermore, our analysis shows that our model can learn fine-grained relevance cues while using coarse-grained supervision, and we demonstrate it in 2 ways. (i) We show that our model can achieve competitive sentence recall while using only paragraph-level relevance supervision. (ii) Traversing towards the finest granularity of relevance, we show that our model is capable of identifying relevance at the token level. To do this, we present a new benchmark TLR-FEVER focusing on token-level interpretability -- humans annotate tokens in relevant evidences they considered essential when making their judgment. Then we measure how similar are these annotations to the tokens our model is focusing on.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了 Claim-Dissector：一种用于事实检查和分析的新型潜在变量模型，给定一个声明和一组检索到的证据，该模型共同学习识别：（i）给定声明的相关证据，（ii）声明的准确性。我们建议以一种可解释的方式分解每个证据的相关概率及其对最终准确性概率的贡献——最终的准确性概率与每个证据相关概率的线性集合成正比。通过这种方式，可以识别证据对最终预测概率的个体贡献。在每个证据的相关概率中，我们的模型可以进一步区分每个相关证据是支持（S）还是反驳（R）主张。这可以量化 S/R 概率对最终判决的贡献程度或检测不同的证据。尽管具有可解释的性质，但与典型的两级系统管道相比，我们的系统在 FEVER 数据集上取得了与最先进的技术相媲美的结果，同时使用的参数明显更少。它还在 FAVIQ 和 RealFC 数据集上设置了新的最先进技术。此外，我们的分析表明，我们的模型可以在使用粗粒度监督的同时学习细粒度的相关线索，并且我们通过两种方式进行了演示。 （i）我们表明，我们的模型可以在仅使用段落级相关性监督的情况下实现竞争性句子回忆。 (ii) 遍历最细的相关性粒度，我们表明我们的模型能够识别令牌级别的相关性。为此，我们提出了一个新的基准 TLR-FEVER，重点关注令牌级别的可解释性——人类在做出判断时在他们认为重要的相关证据中注释令牌。然后我们测量这些注释与我们模型关注的标记的相似程度。

AI.tldr: 提出一种可解释的事实核查系统Claim-Dissector，通过联合重排序和真实性预测，结合证据相关性分析与声明可信度判断。
AI.motivation: 现有事实核查系统多采用两阶段流程，难以直观解释证据与结论的关联性，且参数效率较低，缺乏细粒度的证据分析能力。
AI.method: 设计潜变量模型，将证据相关性概率与真实性预测解耦，通过线性加权证据相关性概率生成最终判断，并区分支持/反驳证据，引入token级可解释性基准TLR-FEVER验证模型关注点。
AI.result: 在FEVER数据集上达到与SOTA相当的效果且参数更少，在FAVIQ和RealFC上取得新SOTA，证明模型能通过段落级监督实现句子级召回，并具备token级解释能力。
AI.conclusion: 该模型通过可解释的联合学习框架，在保持高性能的同时提升了事实核查的透明度，为细粒度证据分析提供了新方法和基准。

result: 在FEVER数据集上达到与SOTA相当的效果且参数更少，在FAVIQ和RealFC上取得新SOTA，证明模型能通过段落级监督实现句子级召回，并具备token级解释能力。
conclusion: 该模型通过可解释的联合学习框架，在保持高性能的同时提升了事实核查的透明度，为细粒度证据分析提供了新方法和基准。
search_text: Claim-Dissector: An Interpretable Fact-Checking System with Joint Re-ranking and Veracity Prediction 声明解析器：具有联合重排序和准确性预测功能的可解释事实检查系统 We present Claim-Dissector: a novel latent variable model for fact-checking and analysis, which given a claim and a set of retrieved evidences jointly learns to identify: (i) the relevant evidences to the given claim, (ii) the veracity of the claim. We propose to disentangle the per-evidence relevance probability and its contribution to the final veracity probability in an interpretable way -- the final veracity probability is proportional to a linear ensemble of per-evidence relevance probabilities. In this way, the individual contributions of evidences towards the final predicted probability can be identified. In per-evidence relevance probability, our model can further distinguish whether each relevant evidence is supporting (S) or refuting (R) the claim. This allows to quantify how much the S/R probability contributes to the final verdict or to detect disagreeing evidence. Despite its interpretable nature, our system achieves results competitive with state-of-the-art on the FEVER dataset, as compared to typical two-stage system pipelines, while using significantly fewer parameters. It also sets new state-of-the-art on FAVIQ and RealFC datasets. Furthermore, our analysis shows that our model can learn fine-grained relevance cues while using coarse-grained supervision, and we demonstrate it in 2 ways. (i) We show that our model can achieve competitive sentence recall while using only paragraph-level relevance supervision. (ii) Traversing towards the finest granularity of relevance, we show that our model is capable of identifying relevance at the token level. To do this, we present a new benchmark TLR-FEVER focusing on token-level interpretability -- humans annotate tokens in relevant evidences they considered essential when making their judgment. Then we measure how similar are these annotations to the tokens our model is focusing on. 我们提出了 Claim-Dissector：一种用于事实检查和分析的新型潜在变量模型，给定一个声明和一组检索到的证据，该模型共同学习识别：（i）给定声明的相关证据，（ii）声明的准确性。我们建议以一种可解释的方式分解每个证据的相关概率及其对最终准确性概率的贡献——最终的准确性概率与每个证据相关概率的线性集合成正比。通过这种方式，可以识别证据对最终预测概率的个体贡献。在每个证据的相关概率中，我们的模型可以进一步区分每个相关证据是支持（S）还是反驳（R）主张。这可以量化 S/R 概率对最终判决的贡献程度或检测不同的证据。尽管具有可解释的性质，但与典型的两级系统管道相比，我们的系统在 FEVER 数据集上取得了与最先进的技术相媲美的结果，同时使用的参数明显更少。它还在 FAVIQ 和 RealFC 数据集上设置了新的最先进技术。此外，我们的分析表明，我们的模型可以在使用粗粒度监督的同时学习细粒度的相关线索，并且我们通过两种方式进行了演示。 （i）我们表明，我们的模型可以在仅使用段落级相关性监督的情况下实现竞争性句子回忆。 (ii) 遍历最细的相关性粒度，我们表明我们的模型能够识别令牌级别的相关性。为此，我们提出了一个新的基准 TLR-FEVER，重点关注令牌级别的可解释性——人类在做出判断时在他们认为重要的相关证据中注释令牌。然后我们测量这些注释与我们模型关注的标记的相似程度。 提出一种可解释的事实核查系统Claim-Dissector，通过联合重排序和真实性预测，结合证据相关性分析与声明可信度判断。 Martin Fajcik,Petr Motlicek,Pavel Smrz cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Adel Nikfarjam,Amirhossein Moosavi,Aneta Neumann,Frank Neumann
title: Computing High-Quality Solutions for the Patient Admission Scheduling Problem using Evolutionary Diversity Optimisation
title_zh: 使用进化多样性优化计算患者入院安排问题的高质量解决方案
abstract: Diversification in a set of solutions has become a hot research topic in the evolutionary computation community. It has been proven beneficial for optimisation problems in several ways, such as computing a diverse set of high-quality solutions and obtaining robustness against imperfect modeling. For the first time in the literature, we adapt the evolutionary diversity optimisation for a real-world combinatorial problem, namely patient admission scheduling. We introduce an evolutionary algorithm to achieve structural diversity in a set of solutions subjected to the quality of each solution. We also introduce a mutation operator biased towards diversity maximisation. Finally, we demonstrate the importance of diversity for the aforementioned problem through a simulation.
summary: Diversification in a set of solutions has become a hot research topic in the evolutionary computation community. It has been proven beneficial for optimisation problems in several ways, such as computing a diverse set of high-quality solutions and obtaining robustness against imperfect modeling. For the first time in the literature, we adapt the evolutionary diversity optimisation for a real-world combinatorial problem, namely patient admission scheduling. We introduce an evolutionary algorithm to achieve structural diversity in a set of solutions subjected to the quality of each solution. We also introduce a mutation operator biased towards diversity maximisation. Finally, we demonstrate the importance of diversity for the aforementioned problem through a simulation.
abstract_zh: 一组解决方案的多样化已成为进化计算界的热门研究课题。事实证明，它在多种方面对优化问题是有益的，例如计算一组不同的高质量解决方案并获得针对不完美建模的鲁棒性。在文献中，我们首次将进化多样性优化应用于现实世界的组合问题，即患者入院安排。我们引入了一种进化算法，以根据每个解决方案的质量在一组解决方案中实现结构多样性。我们还引入了一个偏向于多样性最大化的变异算子。最后，我们通过模拟证明了多样性对于上述问题的重要性。

AI.tldr: 本文首次将进化多样性优化应用于患者住院调度问题，旨在生成高质量且多样化的解决方案。
AI.motivation: 多样化能提升优化问题的鲁棒性并应对建模不完善，但现有研究未将进化多样性优化用于患者住院调度领域。
AI.method: 设计基于进化算法的框架，在保证解质量的同时引入偏向多样性的变异算子，通过模拟验证方法有效性。
AI.result: 实验表明，多样性优化能显著提升患者住院调度问题的解决方案质量与多样性，证明多样性对实际问题的重要性。
AI.conclusion: 进化多样性优化为患者住院调度提供了新思路，未来可探索更高效的多样性保持机制与实际应用验证。

authors_text: Gong Cheng,Xiang Yuan,Xiwen Yao,Kebing Yan,Qinghua Zeng,Xingxing Xie,Junwei Han
title: Towards Large-Scale Small Object Detection: Survey and Benchmarks
title_zh: 迈向大规模小物体检测：调查和基准
comment: in IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence (2023)
comments: in IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence (2023)
abstract: With the rise of deep convolutional neural networks, object detection has achieved prominent advances in past years. However, such prosperity could not camouflage the unsatisfactory situation of Small Object Detection (SOD), one of the notoriously challenging tasks in computer vision, owing to the poor visual appearance and noisy representation caused by the intrinsic structure of small targets. In addition, large-scale dataset for benchmarking small object detection methods remains a bottleneck. In this paper, we first conduct a thorough review of small object detection. Then, to catalyze the development of SOD, we construct two large-scale Small Object Detection dAtasets (SODA), SODA-D and SODA-A, which focus on the Driving and Aerial scenarios respectively. SODA-D includes 24828 high-quality traffic images and 278433 instances of nine categories. For SODA-A, we harvest 2513 high resolution aerial images and annotate 872069 instances over nine classes. The proposed datasets, as we know, are the first-ever attempt to large-scale benchmarks with a vast collection of exhaustively annotated instances tailored for multi-category SOD. Finally, we evaluate the performance of mainstream methods on SODA. We expect the released benchmarks could facilitate the development of SOD and spawn more breakthroughs in this field. Datasets and codes are available at: \url{https://shaunyuan22.github.io/SODA}.

However, such prosperity could not camouflage the unsatisfactory situation of Small Object Detection (SOD), one of the notoriously challenging tasks in computer vision, owing to the poor visual appearance and noisy representation caused by the intrinsic structure of small targets. In addition, large-scale dataset for benchmarking small object detection methods remains a bottleneck. In this paper, we first conduct a thorough review of small object detection. Then, to catalyze the development of SOD, we construct two large-scale Small Object Detection dAtasets (SODA), SODA-D and SODA-A, which focus on the Driving and Aerial scenarios respectively. SODA-D includes 24828 high-quality traffic images and 278433 instances of nine categories. For SODA-A, we harvest 2513 high resolution aerial images and annotate 872069 instances over nine classes. The proposed datasets, as we know, are the first-ever attempt to large-scale benchmarks with a vast collection of exhaustively annotated instances tailored for multi-category SOD. Finally, we evaluate the performance of mainstream methods on SODA. We expect the released benchmarks could facilitate the development of SOD and spawn more breakthroughs in this field. Datasets and codes are available at: \url{https://shaunyuan22.github.io/SODA}.
abstract_zh: 随着深度卷积神经网络的兴起，目标检测在过去几年中取得了显着的进步。然而，这种繁荣并不能掩盖小目标检测（SOD）的不令人满意的情况，这是计算机视觉中众所周知的挑战性任务之一，由于小目标的内在结构导致视觉外观不佳和噪声表示。此外，用于对小目标检测方法进行基准测试的大规模数据集仍然是一个瓶颈。在本文中，我们首先对小物体检测进行彻底的回顾。然后，为了促进 SOD 的发展，我们构建了两个大规模小物体检测数据集（SODA），SODA-D 和 SODA-A，分别专注于驾驶和空中场景。 SODA-D 包含 24828 张高质量交通图像和 9 个类别的 278433 个实例。对于 SODA-A，我们收获了 2513 张高分辨率航拍图像，并对 9 个类别的 872069 个实例进行了注释。据我们所知，所提出的数据集是首次尝试大规模基准测试，其中包含大量专为多类别 SOD 定制的详尽注释实例。最后，我们评估主流方法在 SODA 上的性能。我们期待此次发布的基准测试能够促进SOD的发展，并在该领域带来更多突破。数据集和代码位于：\url{https://shaunyuan22.github.io/SODA}。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过综述和构建大规模基准数据集推动小目标检测（SOD）的发展。
AI.motivation: 小目标检测因目标视觉特征模糊和数据噪声而成为挑战，且缺乏大规模基准数据集阻碍了该领域的发展。
AI.method: 作者系统综述了小目标检测方法，并构建了两个大规模数据集SODA-D（驾驶场景）和SODA-A（航拍场景），包含大量标注实例。
AI.result: 在SODA数据集上评估主流方法，验证了数据集的有效性，为后续研究提供基准。
AI.conclusion: SODA数据集的发布将促进小目标检测技术的突破和算法优化。

authors_text: Russa Biswas,Jan Portisch,Heiko Paulheim,Harald Sack,Mehwish Alam
title: Entity Type Prediction Leveraging Graph Walks and Entity Descriptions
title_zh: 利用图遍历和实体描述的实体类型预测
abstract: The entity type information in Knowledge Graphs (KGs) such as DBpedia, Freebase, etc. is often incomplete due to automated generation or human curation. Entity typing is the task of assigning or inferring the semantic type of an entity in a KG. This paper presents \textit{GRAND}, a novel approach for entity typing leveraging different graph walk strategies in RDF2vec together with textual entity descriptions. RDF2vec first generates graph walks and then uses a language model to obtain embeddings for each node in the graph. This study shows that the walk generation strategy and the embedding model have a significant effect on the performance of the entity typing task. The proposed approach outperforms the baseline approaches on the benchmark datasets DBpedia and FIGER for entity typing in KGs for both fine-grained and coarse-grained classes. The results show that the combination of order-aware RDF2vec variants together with the contextual embeddings of the textual entity descriptions achieve the best results.
summary: The entity type information in Knowledge Graphs (KGs) such as DBpedia, Freebase, etc. is often incomplete due to automated generation or human curation. The proposed approach outperforms the baseline approaches on the benchmark datasets DBpedia and FIGER for entity typing in KGs for both fine-grained and coarse-grained classes. The results show that the combination of order-aware RDF2vec variants together with the contextual embeddings of the textual entity descriptions achieve the best results.
abstract_zh: 由于自动生成或人工管理，知识图谱（KG）（例如 DBpedia、Freebase 等）中的实体类型信息通常不完整。实体类型是分配或推断知识图谱中实体的语义类型的任务。本文提出了 \textit{GRAND}，这是一种利用 RDF2vec 中不同图行走策略以及文本实体描述进行实体类型输入的新颖方法。 RDF2vec 首先生成图行走，然后使用语言模型获得图中每个节点的嵌入。这项研究表明，行走生成策略和嵌入模型对实体打字任务的性能有显着影响。对于细粒度和粗粒度类的 KG 中的实体类型，所提出的方法优于基准数据集 DBpedia 和 Figer 上的基线方法。结果表明，顺序感知的 RDF2vec 变体与文本实体描述的上下文嵌入相结合可达到最佳结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合图遍历策略和实体描述的新型实体类型预测方法GRAND，用于补全知识图谱中的实体类型信息。
AI.motivation: 知识图谱中的实体类型信息常因自动化生成或人工整理而存在缺失，现有方法在细粒度和粗粒度分类任务中表现不足。
AI.method: 通过RDF2vec生成图遍历序列，结合语言模型获取节点嵌入，并融合实体文本描述的上下文嵌入，采用不同遍历策略与嵌入模型组合进行实体类型预测。
AI.result: 在DBpedia和FIGER基准数据集上，GRAND在细粒度和粗粒度分类任务中均优于基线方法，验证了图遍历策略与文本嵌入结合的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 基于顺序感知的RDF2vec变体与文本描述上下文嵌入的结合，能显著提升知识图谱实体类型预测的准确性。

search_text: Entity Type Prediction Leveraging Graph Walks and Entity Descriptions 利用图遍历和实体描述的实体类型预测 The entity type information in Knowledge Graphs (KGs) such as DBpedia, Freebase, etc. is often incomplete due to automated generation or human curation. Entity typing is the task of assigning or inferring the semantic type of an entity in a KG. This paper presents \textit{GRAND}, a novel approach for entity typing leveraging different graph walk strategies in RDF2vec together with textual entity descriptions. RDF2vec first generates graph walks and then uses a language model to obtain embeddings for each node in the graph. This study shows that the walk generation strategy and the embedding model have a significant effect on the performance of the entity typing task. The proposed approach outperforms the baseline approaches on the benchmark datasets DBpedia and FIGER for entity typing in KGs for both fine-grained and coarse-grained classes. The results show that the combination of order-aware RDF2vec variants together with the contextual embeddings of the textual entity descriptions achieve the best results. 由于自动生成或人工管理，知识图谱（KG）（例如 DBpedia、Freebase 等）中的实体类型信息通常不完整。实体类型是分配或推断知识图谱中实体的语义类型的任务。本文提出了 \textit{GRAND}，这是一种利用 RDF2vec 中不同图行走策略以及文本实体描述进行实体类型输入的新颖方法。 RDF2vec 首先生成图行走，然后使用语言模型获得图中每个节点的嵌入。这项研究表明，行走生成策略和嵌入模型对实体打字任务的性能有显着影响。对于细粒度和粗粒度类的 KG 中的实体类型，所提出的方法优于基准数据集 DBpedia 和 Figer 上的基线方法。结果表明，顺序感知的 RDF2vec 变体与文本实体描述的上下文嵌入相结合可达到最佳结果。 本文提出了一种结合图遍历策略和实体描述的新型实体类型预测方法GRAND，用于补全知识图谱中的实体类型信息。 Russa Biswas,Jan Portisch,Heiko Paulheim,Harald Sack,Mehwish Alam cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Hao Sun,Hongyi Wang,Jiaqing Liu,Yen-Wei Chen,Lanfen Lin
title: CubeMLP: An MLP-based Model for Multimodal Sentiment Analysis and Depression Estimation
title_zh: CubeMLP：基于 MLP 的多模态情感分析和抑郁估计模型
abstract: Multimodal sentiment analysis and depression estimation are two important research topics that aim to predict human mental states using multimodal data. Previous research has focused on developing effective fusion strategies for exchanging and integrating mind-related information from different modalities. Some MLP-based techniques have recently achieved considerable success in a variety of computer vision tasks. Inspired by this, we explore multimodal approaches with a feature-mixing perspective in this study. To this end, we introduce CubeMLP, a multimodal feature processing framework based entirely on MLP. CubeMLP consists of three independent MLP units, each of which has two affine transformations. CubeMLP accepts all relevant modality features as input and mixes them across three axes. After extracting the characteristics using CubeMLP, the mixed multimodal features are flattened for task predictions. Our experiments are conducted on sentiment analysis datasets: CMU-MOSI and CMU-MOSEI, and depression estimation dataset: AVEC2019. The results show that CubeMLP can achieve state-of-the-art performance with a much lower computing cost.
summary: Multimodal sentiment analysis and depression estimation are two important research topics that aim to predict human mental states using multimodal data. Previous research has focused on developing effective fusion strategies for exchanging and integrating mind-related information from different modalities. Some MLP-based techniques have recently achieved considerable success in a variety of computer vision tasks. Inspired by this, we explore multimodal approaches with a feature-mixing perspective in this study. To this end, we introduce CubeMLP, a multimodal feature processing framework based entirely on MLP. CubeMLP consists of three independent MLP units, each of which has two affine transformations. CubeMLP accepts all relevant modality features as input and mixes them across three axes. After extracting the characteristics using CubeMLP, the mixed multimodal features are flattened for task predictions. Our experiments are conducted on sentiment analysis datasets: CMU-MOSI and CMU-MOSEI, and depression estimation dataset: AVEC2019. The results show that CubeMLP can achieve state-of-the-art performance with a much lower computing cost.
abstract_zh: 多模态情感分析和抑郁估计是两个重要的研究课题，旨在利用多模态数据预测人类心理状态。先前的研究重点是开发有效的融合策略，以交换和整合来自不同模式的思维相关信息。一些基于 MLP 的技术最近在各种计算机视觉任务中取得了相当大的成功。受此启发，我们在本研究中从特征混合的角度探索多模式方法。为此，我们引入了CubeMLP，一个完全基于MLP的多模态特征处理框架。 CubeMLP 由三个独立的 MLP 单元组成，每个单元都有两个仿射变换。 CubeMLP 接受所有相关模态特征作为输入，并将它们跨三个轴混合。使用 CubeMLP 提取特征后，混合多模态特征被展平以用于任务预测。我们的实验是在情感分析数据集：CMU-MOSI 和 CMU-MOSEI 以及抑郁估计数据集：AVEC2019 上进行的。结果表明，CubeMLP 可以以低得多的计算成本实现最先进的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CubeMLP，一种基于多层感知机（MLP）的多模态特征处理框架，用于情感分析和抑郁估计任务。
AI.motivation: 现有研究多关注多模态信息融合策略，而MLP在计算机视觉任务中表现出色，因此探索基于MLP的多模态特征混合方法以提升效率与性能。
AI.method: CubeMLP包含三个独立MLP单元，每个单元包含两次仿射变换，通过三轴混合多模态特征后展平输出，用于情感分析和抑郁估计任务。
AI.result: 在CMU-MOSI、CMU-MOSEI和AVEC2019数据集上，CubeMLP在保持较低计算成本的同时达到最先进性能。
AI.conclusion: CubeMLP通过纯MLP结构实现多模态特征高效混合，在情感分析和抑郁估计中展现出优越性与计算优势。

authors_text: Ruozhen He,Qihua Dong,Jiaying Lin,Rynson W. H. Lau
title: Weakly-Supervised Camouflaged Object Detection with Scribble Annotations
title_zh: 具有涂鸦注释的弱监督伪装目标检测
comment: Accepted to AAAI 2023. The code and dataset are available at https://github.com/dddraxxx/Weakly-Supervised-Camouflaged-Object-Detection-with-Scribble-Annotations
comments: Accepted to AAAI 2023. The code and dataset are available at https://github.com/dddraxxx/Weakly-Supervised-Camouflaged-Object-Detection-with-Scribble-Annotations
abstract: Existing camouflaged object detection (COD) methods rely heavily on large-scale datasets with pixel-wise annotations. However, due to the ambiguous boundary, annotating camouflage objects pixel-wisely is very time-consuming and labor-intensive, taking ~60mins to label one image. In this paper, we propose the first weakly-supervised COD method, using scribble annotations as supervision. To achieve this, we first relabel 4,040 images in existing camouflaged object datasets with scribbles, which takes ~10s to label one image. As scribble annotations only describe the primary structure of objects without details, for the network to learn to localize the boundaries of camouflaged objects, we propose a novel consistency loss composed of two parts: a cross-view loss to attain reliable consistency over different images, and an inside-view loss to maintain consistency inside a single prediction map. Besides, we observe that humans use semantic information to segment regions near the boundaries of camouflaged objects. Hence, we further propose a feature-guided loss, which includes visual features directly extracted from images and semantically significant features captured by the model. Finally, we propose a novel network for COD via scribble learning on structural information and semantic relations. Our network has two novel modules: the local-context contrasted (LCC) module, which mimics visual inhibition to enhance image contrast/sharpness and expand the scribbles into potential camouflaged regions, and the logical semantic relation (LSR) module, which analyzes the semantic relation to determine the regions representing the camouflaged object. Experimental results show that our model outperforms relevant SOTA methods on three COD benchmarks with an average improvement of 11.0% on MAE, 3.2% on S-measure, 2.5% on E-measure, and 4.4% on weighted F-measure.
summary: Existing camouflaged object detection (COD) methods rely heavily on large-scale datasets with pixel-wise annotations. However, due to the ambiguous boundary, annotating camouflage objects pixel-wisely is very time-consuming and labor-intensive, taking ~60mins to label one image. In this paper, we propose the first weakly-supervised COD method, using scribble annotations as supervision. To achieve this, we first relabel 4,040 images in existing camouflaged object datasets with scribbles, which takes ~10s to label one image. As scribble annotations only describe the primary structure of objects without details, for the network to learn to localize the boundaries of camouflaged objects, we propose a novel consistency loss composed of two parts: a cross-view loss to attain reliable consistency over different images, and an inside-view loss to maintain consistency inside a single prediction map. Besides, we observe that humans use semantic information to segment regions near the boundaries of camouflaged objects. Hence, we further propose a feature-guided loss, which includes visual features directly extracted from images and semantically significant features captured by the model. Finally, we propose a novel network for COD via scribble learning on structural information and semantic relations. Our network has two novel modules: the local-context contrasted (LCC) module, which mimics visual inhibition to enhance image contrast/sharpness and expand the scribbles into potential camouflaged regions, and the logical semantic relation (LSR) module, which analyzes the semantic relation to determine the regions representing the camouflaged object. Experimental results show that our model outperforms relevant SOTA methods on three COD benchmarks with an average improvement of 11.0% on MAE, 3.2% on S-measure, 2.5% on E-measure, and 4.4% on weighted F-measure.
abstract_zh: 现有的伪装目标检测（COD）方法严重依赖于具有像素级注释的大规模数据集。然而，由于边界不明确，以像素方式注释伪装对象非常耗时且费力，标记一张图像大约需要 60 分钟。在本文中，我们提出了第一个弱监督 COD 方法，使用涂鸦注释作为监督。为了实现这一目标，我们首先用涂鸦重新标记现有伪装对象数据集中的 4,040 张图像，这大约需要 10 秒来标记一张图像。由于涂鸦注释仅描述了对象的主要结构而没有细节，为了使网络学习定位伪装对象的边界，我们提出了一种新颖的一致性损失，该损失由两部分组成：跨视图损失以在不同图像上获得可靠的一致性，以及内部视图损失以维持单个预测图内的一致性。此外，我们观察到人类使用语义信息来分割伪装物体边界附近的区域。因此，我们进一步提出了一种特征引导损失，其中包括直接从图像中提取的视觉特征和模型捕获的语义上重要的特征。最后，我们通过结构信息和语义关系的涂鸦学习提出了一种新颖的 COD 网络。我们的网络有两个新颖的模块：局部上下文对比（LCC）模块，它模仿视觉抑制以增强图像对比度/清晰度并将涂鸦扩展到潜在的伪装区域；逻辑语义关系（LSR）模块，它分析语义关系以确定代表伪装对象的区域。实验结果表明，我们的模型在三个 COD 基准上优于相关的 SOTA 方法，MAE 平均提高 11.0%，S-measure 平均提高 3.2%，E-measure 平均提高 2.5%，加权 F-measure 平均提高 4.4%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于涂鸦标注的弱监督伪装目标检测方法，以减少传统像素级标注的高成本。
AI.motivation: 现有伪装目标检测方法依赖耗时的像素级标注（每张图像约60分钟），而涂鸦标注可将标注时间缩短至10秒，但需解决边界定位和语义信息利用问题。
AI.method: 通过涂鸦重标注数据集，设计包含跨视图损失和内部视图损失的一致性损失函数，结合特征引导损失（融合视觉特征与语义特征），并提出局部对比模块（LCC）和逻辑语义关系模块（LSR）增强结构信息与语义关联学习。
AI.result: 在三个COD基准数据集上，模型在MAE、S-measure、E-measure和加权F-measure指标上分别平均提升11.0%、3.2%、2.5%和4.4%，优于现有SOTA方法。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过弱监督学习有效解决了伪装目标检测中边界定位与语义关联问题，为减少标注成本提供了新思路。

search_text: Weakly-Supervised Camouflaged Object Detection with Scribble Annotations 具有涂鸦注释的弱监督伪装目标检测 Existing camouflaged object detection (COD) methods rely heavily on large-scale datasets with pixel-wise annotations. However, due to the ambiguous boundary, annotating camouflage objects pixel-wisely is very time-consuming and labor-intensive, taking ~60mins to label one image. In this paper, we propose the first weakly-supervised COD method, using scribble annotations as supervision. To achieve this, we first relabel 4,040 images in existing camouflaged object datasets with scribbles, which takes ~10s to label one image. As scribble annotations only describe the primary structure of objects without details, for the network to learn to localize the boundaries of camouflaged objects, we propose a novel consistency loss composed of two parts: a cross-view loss to attain reliable consistency over different images, and an inside-view loss to maintain consistency inside a single prediction map. Besides, we observe that humans use semantic information to segment regions near the boundaries of camouflaged objects. Hence, we further propose a feature-guided loss, which includes visual features directly extracted from images and semantically significant features captured by the model. Finally, we propose a novel network for COD via scribble learning on structural information and semantic relations. Our network has two novel modules: the local-context contrasted (LCC) module, which mimics visual inhibition to enhance image contrast/sharpness and expand the scribbles into potential camouflaged regions, and the logical semantic relation (LSR) module, which analyzes the semantic relation to determine the regions representing the camouflaged object. Experimental results show that our model outperforms relevant SOTA methods on three COD benchmarks with an average improvement of 11.0% on MAE, 3.2% on S-measure, 2.5% on E-measure, and 4.4% on weighted F-measure. 现有的伪装目标检测（COD）方法严重依赖于具有像素级注释的大规模数据集。然而，由于边界不明确，以像素方式注释伪装对象非常耗时且费力，标记一张图像大约需要 60 分钟。在本文中，我们提出了第一个弱监督 COD 方法，使用涂鸦注释作为监督。为了实现这一目标，我们首先用涂鸦重新标记现有伪装对象数据集中的 4,040 张图像，这大约需要 10 秒来标记一张图像。由于涂鸦注释仅描述了对象的主要结构而没有细节，为了使网络学习定位伪装对象的边界，我们提出了一种新颖的一致性损失，该损失由两部分组成：跨视图损失以在不同图像上获得可靠的一致性，以及内部视图损失以维持单个预测图内的一致性。此外，我们观察到人类使用语义信息来分割伪装物体边界附近的区域。因此，我们进一步提出了一种特征引导损失，其中包括直接从图像中提取的视觉特征和模型捕获的语义上重要的特征。最后，我们通过结构信息和语义关系的涂鸦学习提出了一种新颖的 COD 网络。我们的网络有两个新颖的模块：局部上下文对比（LCC）模块，它模仿视觉抑制以增强图像对比度/清晰度并将涂鸦扩展到潜在的伪装区域；逻辑语义关系（LSR）模块，它分析语义关系以确定代表伪装对象的区域。实验结果表明，我们的模型在三个 COD 基准上优于相关的 SOTA 方法，MAE 平均提高 11.0%，S-measure 平均提高 3.2%，E-measure 平均提高 2.5%，加权 F-measure 平均提高 4.4%。 本文提出了一种基于涂鸦标注的弱监督伪装目标检测方法，以减少传统像素级标注的高成本。 Ruozhen He,Qihua Dong,Jiaying Lin,Rynson W. H. Lau cs.CV

authors_text: Florent Nauleau,Fabien Vivodtzev,Thibault Bridel-Bertomeu,Heloise Beaugendre,Julien Tierny
title: Topological Analysis of Ensembles of Hydrodynamic Turbulent Flows -- An Experimental Study
title_zh: 水动力湍流系综的拓扑分析——实验研究
abstract: This application paper presents a comprehensive experimental evaluation of the suitability of Topological Data Analysis (TDA) for the quantitative comparison of turbulent flows. Specifically, our study documents the usage of the persistence diagram of the maxima of flow enstrophy (an established vorticity indicator), for the topological representation of 180 ensemble members, generated by a coarse sampling of the parameter space of five numerical solvers. We document five main hypotheses reported by domain experts, describing their expectations regarding the variability of the flows generated by the distinct solver configurations. We contribute three evaluation protocols to assess the validation of the above hypotheses by two comparison measures: (i) a standard distance used in scientific imaging (the L2 norm) and (ii) an established topological distance between persistence diagrams (the L2-Wasserstein metric). Extensive experiments on the input ensemble demonstrate the superiority of the topological distance (ii) to report as close to each other flows which are expected to be similar by domain experts, due to the configuration of their vortices. Overall, the insights reported by our study bring an experimental evidence of the suitability of TDA for representing and comparing turbulent flows, thereby providing to the fluid dynamics community confidence for its usage in future work. Also, our flow data and evaluation protocols provide to the TDA community an application-approved benchmark for the evaluation and design of further topological distances.
summary: This application paper presents a comprehensive experimental evaluation of the suitability of Topological Data Analysis (TDA) for the quantitative comparison of turbulent flows. Specifically, our study documents the usage of the persistence diagram of the maxima of flow enstrophy (an established vorticity indicator), for the topological representation of 180 ensemble members, generated by a coarse sampling of the parameter space of five numerical solvers. We document five main hypotheses reported by domain experts, describing their expectations regarding the variability of the flows generated by the distinct solver configurations. We contribute three evaluation protocols to assess the validation of the above hypotheses by two comparison measures: (i) a standard distance used in scientific imaging (the L2 norm) and (ii) an established topological distance between persistence diagrams (the L2-Wasserstein metric). Extensive experiments on the input ensemble demonstrate the superiority of the topological distance (ii) to report as close to each other flows which are expected to be similar by domain experts, due to the configuration of their vortices. Overall, the insights reported by our study bring an experimental evidence of the suitability of TDA for representing and comparing turbulent flows, thereby providing to the fluid dynamics community confidence for its usage in future work. Also, our flow data and evaluation protocols provide to the TDA community an application-approved benchmark for the evaluation and design of further topological distances.
abstract_zh: 本应用论文对拓扑数据分析 (TDA) 对于湍流定量比较的适用性进行了全面的实验评估。具体来说，我们的研究记录了流熵最大值持久性图（一种已建立的涡度指标）的使用，用于 180 个系综成员的拓扑表示，由五个数值求解器的参数空间的粗采样生成。我们记录了领域专家报告的五个主要假设，描述了他们对不同求解器配置生成的流的可变性的期望。我们贡献了三个评估协议，通过两种比较措施来评估上述假设的有效性：（i）科学成像中使用的标准距离（L2范数）和（ii）持久性图之间已建立的拓扑距离（L2-Wasserstein度量）。对输入系综的大量实验证明了拓扑距离 (ii) 报告彼此接近的流的优越性，由于其涡流的配置，领域专家预计这些流是相似的。总体而言，我们的研究报告的见解提供了 TDA 适用于表示和比较湍流的实验证据，从而为流体动力学界提供了对其在未来工作中使用的信心。此外，我们的流数据和评估协议为 TDA 社区提供了应用程序批准的基准，用于评估和设计进一步的拓扑距离。

AI.tldr: 该论文通过实验评估拓扑数据分析（TDA）在定量比较湍流中的适用性，利用流体涡旋强度的持久性图谱分析180组湍流数据。
AI.motivation: 湍流的复杂性和多变性使得传统方法难以有效比较不同流体动力学求解器生成的流场，需探索更稳健的量化分析工具。
AI.method: 采用流体涡旋强度（enstrophy）最大值的持久性图谱作为拓扑表示，结合L2范数与L2-Wasserstein度量两种距离指标，验证五个领域专家提出的假设。
AI.result: 实验表明，拓扑距离（L2-Wasserstein）能更准确识别领域专家认为相似的流场配置，证明TDA在湍流比较中的优越性。
AI.conclusion: TDA为流体力学提供了有效的湍流表征与比较方法，并为TDA社区建立了经应用验证的基准数据集与评估协议。
